key_insight: Pr9Ge16 forms a newly reported orthorhombic Fdd2 crystal structure with self-organized Ge vacancy order, and its magnetic easy axis is along the b direction.
engineering_relevance: The discovery links vacancy ordering to anisotropic magnetism and metallic transport, offering a structural design route for tuning properties in rare-earth germanides.
method: High-temperature flux growth of Pr9Ge16 single crystals
Single-crystal structure determination of the orthorhombic Fdd2 phase with ordered Ge vacancies
Magnetization, resistivity, and Hall measurements to probe anisotropy, transport, and carrier type
evidence_signal: The abstract reports metallic resistivity with an anomaly at TC = 14.3 K, electron-like carriers near 10^27 m^-3, and field suppression of the magnetic order by about 0.4 T along the b axis.
watch_item: The findings are specific to one newly synthesized phase, so the vacancy-order mechanism and property trends may not directly generalize without further composition and field-direction studies.
paper_title: The self-organized vacancy order in Pr$_9$Ge$_{16}$
